description: Graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor. The background is a deep blue sky with clouds over a color photograph of the Frances Perkins DOL headquarters building in Washington, D.C. Gold numerals fill the upper center: 'Since' (in a white script font) above '1913' (large gold serif), with the white all-caps subhead 'SERVING THE AMERICAN WORKER' beneath, and the small DOL seal centered below the text. The building facade carries a giant U.S. flag hanging vertically at center, red-white-and-blue bunting along the lower floors, and rows of small American flags along the plaza. Two large building-banner portraits are draped on the facade: at left a stylized portrait of Donald Trump with an 'AMERICA 250' mark and the slogan 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'; at right a matching banner with a portrait (Frances Perkins) and the same 'AMERICA 250 / AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' slogan. The carved building name 'FRANCES PERKINS, DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING' is visible. The tweet text reads: 'Today, we renew our commitment to helping @POTUS put AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST.'
notable_text: Since 1913 - SERVING THE AMERICAN WORKER; AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST; AMERICA 250
